title: Write DSL.md: grammar, shorthands, examples, rationale
description: Analyze the current ast-search CLI (main.ts, search.ts, file.ts) and write DSL.md at the repo root. The spec must define: (1) formal EBNF grammar, (2) shorthand aliases for common expression types (e.g. 'this' -> ThisExpression, 'await' -> AwaitExpression), (3) at minimum 8 worked examples covering bare identifier search, scope>expression containment, wildcard, negation, and logical composition, (4) design rationale for each syntax choice. Output is a human-readable Markdown doc. No implementation code in this task. npm test must still pass.

title: Implement src/query.ts with parseQuery, Query types, and full unit tests in src/__tests__/query.test.ts
description: Implement parseQuery(query: string): Query that parses all grammar forms from DSL.md: scope-query (identifier > expr-clause), bare-expr (expr-clause starting with shorthand or PascalCase), bare-ident (plain identifier). Query type must capture: kind (scope|bare-expr|bare-ident), scope (string|null), wildcard flag, and the expression tree (and-clauses of or-clauses). Unit tests must cover: scope-query, bare-ident, bare-expr shorthands, PascalCase babel-type, negation, &&, ||, wildcard scope, import() shorthand, unknown bare token fallthrough, invalid expression-position token (parse error). npm test must pass.

title: Implement src/walk.ts with walkRepoFiles and tests
description: Implement walkRepoFiles(dir: string): AsyncIterable<string> in src/walk.ts. Recursively yields all .js .ts .jsx .tsx .mjs .cjs .vue file paths, skipping node_modules and hidden directories (dot-prefixed). Tests in src/__tests__/walk.test.ts using memfs mock: deep recursion, node_modules exclusion, empty dir, mixed extensions. Acceptance: all tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Implement expression-containment search: Match type + runQuery + unit tests
description: Implement searchForExpression in src/search.ts (currently commented out) and export a runQuery(query: Query, ast: File): Match[] function. Match = { file: string; line: number; col: number; text: string }. Logic: BareIdent → find root nodes (existing searchForRootNodes), return their locations. BareExpr → walk entire AST, return nodes whose .type matches the ExprClause. ScopeQuery → find scope root nodes, then depth-first search within each root node body for matching expression types. Wire through Predicate negation. Add src/__tests__/search.test.ts (or extend existing) with unit tests using fixture ASTs from memfs setup. All tests must pass, tsc must be clean.

title: Refactor main.ts: bare query CLI + repo walk integration
description: Refactor main.ts to accept a bare positional query string (e.g. 'ast-search \'setup > ThisExpression\''), replacing the old -f/-r/-s flags. Wire together: (1) walkRepoFiles(process.cwd()) from src/walk.ts, (2) parseFile() from src/file.ts, (3) parseQuery() from src/query.ts, (4) runQuery() from src/search.ts. Stream results to stdout as 'file:line:col: matched text'. Add an integration test that exercises the full pipeline against the existing fixture files. All existing tests must continue to pass. tsc must be clean.

title: Implement output formatter with ANSI highlighting and TTY detection
description: Create src/output.ts that formats Match[] as 'file:line:col: <source text>' lines. ANSI-highlight the matched token(s) when stdout is a TTY; plain text when piped. Wire into main.ts so searchRepo output is printed via this formatter. Tests in src/__tests__/output.test.ts must cover: (1) color path when isTTY=true, (2) no-color path when isTTY=false, (3) correct file:line:col prefix format, (4) source text snippet included in output. Acceptance criteria: npm test passes (98+ tests), tsc clean, running ast-search '<query>' in a terminal shows highlighted output.
